questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $\int \frac{a \cos x-2 \sin x}{b \sin x+5 \cos x} d x=\frac{7}{41} x+\frac{22}{7} \log$ $|b \sin x+5 \cos x|+C,(a>0, b>0)$, then $\int \frac{d x}{b+a \cos x}=$
questions.solution:
We have,
$\int \frac{a \cos x-2 \sin x}{b \sin x+5 \cos x} d x=\frac{7}{41} x+\frac{22}{41}$
$\begin{aligned} & \log |(b \sin x+5 \cos x)|+C \\ & \text { Now, let } a \cos x-2 \sin x=\lambda(b \sin x+5 \cos x) \\ & \qquad+\mu(b \cos x-5 \sin x)\end{aligned}$
$\therefore a=5 \lambda+\mu b$ and $-2=b \lambda-5 \mu$
But it is given, $\lambda=\frac{7}{41}$ and $\mu=\frac{22}{41}$
$a=3, b=4$
$\int \frac{d x}{b+a \cos x}=\int \frac{d x}{4+3 \cos x}$
$=\int \frac{\left(1+\tan ^2 \frac{x}{2}\right) d x}{4\left(1+\tan ^2 \frac{x}{2}\right)+3\left(1-\tan ^2 \frac{x}{2}\right)}$
$\begin{aligned} &=\int \frac{\sec ^2 x / 2}{7+\tan ^2 x / 2} d x \\ & \tan \frac{x}{2}=t \Rightarrow \sec ^2 \frac{x}{2} d x=2 d t \\ & \frac{d x}{b+a \cos x}=2 \int \frac{d t}{7+t^2} \\ &=\frac{2}{\sqrt{7}} \tan ^{-1} \frac{t}{\sqrt{7}}+C \\ &=\frac{2}{\sqrt{7}} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\tan x / 2}{\sqrt{7}}\right)+C\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If <math> <mrow> <mo>∫</mo> <mfrac> <mrow> <mtext>3 sin x + 2 cos x</mtext> </mrow> <mrow> <mtext>3 cos x + 2 sin x</mtext> </mrow> </mfrac> <mtext>dx = ax + b</mtext> <mtext mathvariant="italic"> ln </mtext> <mfenced close="|" open="|" separators=","> <mrow> <mtext>2 sin x + 3 cos x</mtext> </mrow> </mfenced> <mo>+</mo> <mtext>C</mtext> </mrow> </math>, then
questions.solution:
Differentiating both sides, we get

 3 sin x + 2 cos x 3 cos x + 2 sin x = = a + b(2 cos x - 3 sin x) (2 sin x + 3 cos x)

= sin x (2a - 3b) + cos x (3a + 2b) 3 cos x + 2 sin x

Comparing like terms on both sides, we get

3 = 2 a - 3 b, 2 = 3a + 2b ⇒ a = 1 2 1 3 , b = - 1 5 3 9
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>∫</mo><msup><mi>e</mi><mi>x</mi></msup><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mfrac><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="italic">sin</mi><mo>⁡</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="italic">cos</mi><mo>⁡</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfrac></mfenced><mi> </mi><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo></math>
questions.solution:
Let, I=∫ex2+sin⁡2x1+cos⁡2x dx

We know that sin2x=2sinxcosx & cos2x=2cos2x-1

Using the above formulas we can write

I=∫ex2+2sinxcosx2cos2xdx

I=∫ex21+sinxcosx2cos2xdx

I=∫ex1cos2x+sinxcosxcos2xdx
I= ∫ e x ( tanx+ sec 2 x )dx

We know that ∫exfx+f'xdx=ex·f+C

Using the above formula we can write

I= e x tanx+C
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: $\int x \log x d x$ is equal to
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} \int \frac{x}{\Pi} \log _{I} x d x &=\log x \cdot \frac{x^{2}}{2}-\int \frac{1}{x} \cdot \frac{x^{2}}{2} d x \\ &=\frac{x^{2}}{2} \log x-\frac{1}{2} \frac{x^{2}}{2}+c \\ &=\frac{x^{2}}{4}(2 \log x-1)+c \end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: $\begin{aligned} & \text { If } \frac{4 x^2+5 x^4+7}{\left(x^2+1\right)\left(x^4+x^2+1\right)}=\frac{A x+B}{x^2+1} \\ & +\frac{C x^3+D x^2+E x+F}{x^4+x^2+1}, \text { then } \\ & B+2(D+F+E)-C \cdot A=\end{aligned}$
questions.solution:
We have,
$\begin{aligned}
& \frac{4 x^2+5 x^4+7}{\left(x^2+1\right)\left(x^4+x^2+1\right)}=\frac{A x+B}{x^2+1}+\frac{C x^3+D x^2+E x+F}{x^4+x^2+1} \\
& \Rightarrow 4 x^2+5 x^4+7=(A x+B)\left(x^4+x^2+1\right) \\
& +\left(C x^3+D x^2+E x+F\right)\left(x^2+1\right) \\
& \Rightarrow 5 x^4+4 x^2+7=A x^5+A x^3+A x+B x^4+B x^2 \\
& +B+C x^5+C x^3+D x^4+D x^2+E x^3+E x+F x^2+F \\
& \Rightarrow 5 x^4+4 x^2+7=(A+C) x^5+(B+D) x^4 \\
& +(A+C+E) x^3+(B+D+F) x^2+(A+E) x+(B+F) \\
\end{aligned}$
On comparing, we get
$\begin{aligned}
& A+C=0 \\
& B+D=5
\end{aligned}$
$\begin{array}{r}
A+C+E=0 \\
B+D+F=4 \\
A+E=0 \\
B+F=7
\end{array}$
On solving, we get
$\begin{gathered}
A=C=E=0, B=8 . D=-3, F=-1 \\
\therefore B+2(D+F+E)-C \cdot A=8+2(-3-1+0)-0 \times 0 \\
=8-8=0
\end{gathered}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>∫</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><mo>(</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><msup><mo>)</mo><mn>5</mn></msup><mo>(</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><msup><mo>)</mo><mn>4</mn></msup></mrow></mfrac><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><munderover><mo>∑</mo><mrow><mi>r</mi><mo>=</mo><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></munderover><msub><mi>A</mi><mi>r</mi></msub><msup><mfenced><mfrac><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfrac></mfenced><mi>r</mi></msup><mo>+</mo><munderover><mo>∑</mo><mrow><mi>r</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mn>3</mn></munderover><msub><mi>A</mi><mi>r</mi></msub><msup><mfenced><mfrac><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfrac></mfenced><mi>r</mi></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>B</mi><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>,</mo></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo></math>
questions.solution:
I=∫1x-25.x-14dx

⇒ I=∫dxx-29.x-1x-24

Let x-1x-2=t

⇒ x-2.1-x-1.1x-22dx=dt

⇒ -1x-22dx=dt   ...1

Also, x-1x-2=t

⇒ x-1=tx-2t

⇒ x=2t-1t-1

⇒ x-2=1t-1  ...2

∴ I=∫-dt1t-17t4

⇒ I=∫1-t7t4dt

⇒ I=∫1-c17t+c27t2-c37t3+......+c67.t6-c77t7t4dt

⇒ I=∫1t4dt-c17∫dtt3+c27∫dtt2-c37∫1tdt+....-c77∫t3dt

⇒ I=t-4+1-4+1-7 t-3+1-3+1+c27t-2+1-2+1-c37logt+...-t44+c

  ⇒ I=-13x-1x-2-3+72x-1x-2-2-c37logx-1x-2+.....- 14x-1x-24+c

⇒ I=-13x-2x-13+72x-2x-12+c27x-2x-1+c47x-2x-1-1+c37logx-2-logx-1....-14x-2x-1-4+c  ...1

∵ Given that

∫1(x-2)5(x-1)4dx=∑r=-4-1Arx-2x-1r+∑r=13Arx-2x-1r+Bfx

∴We get fx=logx-2-logx-1
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>∫</mo><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mi>cos</mi><mo>⁡</mo><mi>x</mi><mi> </mi><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>2</mn></mfrac><msup><mfenced open="{" close="}" separators="|"><mrow><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mi>x</mi></mfenced></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>c</mi><mo>,</mo></math> then <math><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is
questions.solution:
Given, ∫fxcos⁡x dx=12fx2+c

Differentiate both sides we get, 

fxcos⁡x=12⋅2fxf′(x)

Then, f′x=cos⁡x

Integrate both the sides we get, 

fx=sin⁡x+c
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.q: $\int e^{x} \frac{(x-1)}{x^{2}} d x$ is equal to
questions.solution:
$\int e^{x}\left(\frac{x-1}{x^{2}}\right) d x$
$\quad=\int e^{x}\left(\frac{1}{x}-\frac{1}{x^{2}}\right) d x$
$=\frac{e^{x}}{x}+c\left[\because \int e^{x}\left[f(x)+f^{\prime}(x)\right] d x=e^{x} f(x)+c\right]$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The value of $\int e^{x}\left[\frac{1+\sin x}{1+\cos x}\right] d x$ is
questions.solution:
Let $I=\int e^{x}\left[\frac{1+\sin x}{1+\cos x}\right] d x$
$\begin{aligned}=& \int\left\{\frac{e^{x}}{(1+\cos x)}+\frac{e^{x} \sin x}{(1+\cos x)}\right\} d x \\=& \int \frac{e^{x}}{2 \cos ^{2} \frac{x}{2}} d x+\int \frac{e^{x} \cdot 2 \sin \frac{x}{2} \cdot \cos \frac{x}{2}}{2 \cos ^{2} \frac{x}{2}} \cdot d x \\=& \frac{1}{2} \int e^{x} \cdot \sec ^{2} \frac{x}{2} \cdot d x+\int \underset{\mathbb{I}}{e^{x} \tan } \frac{x}{2} \cdot d x \\=& \frac{1}{2} \int e^{x} \cdot \sec ^{2} \frac{x}{2} \cdot d x \\+\left\{\tan \frac{x}{2} \cdot e^{x}-\int \frac{1}{2} \cdot \sec ^{2} \frac{x}{2} \cdot e^{x} d x\right\} \end{aligned}$
(using integral by parts)
$=\frac{1}{2} \int e^{x} \cdot \sec ^{2} \frac{x}{2} d x+e^{x} \cdot \tan \frac{x}{2}-\frac{1}{2}$
$\int e^{x} \cdot \sec ^{2} \frac{x}{2} \cdot d x$
$=e^{x} \cdot \tan \frac{x}{2}+C$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $\int \frac{x^3 \mathrm{~d} x}{\sqrt{1+x^2}}=\mathrm{a}\left(1+x^2\right) \sqrt{1+x^2}+\mathrm{b} \sqrt{1+x^2}+\mathrm{c}$ (where $\mathrm{c}$ is a constant of integration), then the value of $3 \mathrm{ab}$ is
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \text { Put } 1+x^2=\mathrm{t}^2 \\ & \Rightarrow 2 x \mathrm{~d} x=2 \mathrm{t} \mathrm{dt} \\ & \Rightarrow x \mathrm{~d} x=\mathrm{t} d \mathrm{t} \\ & \quad \int \frac{x^3 \mathrm{~d} x}{\sqrt{1+x^2}} \\ & =\int \frac{\mathrm{t}^2-1}{\mathrm{t}} \cdot \mathrm{t} d \mathrm{t} \\ & =\int\left(\mathrm{t}^2-1\right) \mathrm{dt} \\ & =\frac{\mathrm{t}^3}{3}-\mathrm{t}+\mathrm{c} \\ & =\frac{\left(1+x^2\right)^{\frac{3}{2}}}{3}-\sqrt{1+x^2}+\mathrm{c} \\ & =\frac{1}{3}\left(1+x^2\right) \sqrt{1+x^2}-\sqrt{1+x^2}+\mathrm{c}\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} \therefore \quad & a=\frac{1}{3}, b=-1 \\ & \Rightarrow 3 a b=-1\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $\int \frac{\sqrt{1-x^4}}{x^7} d x=f(x)\left\{\sqrt{1-x^4}\right\}^n+C$, then $(f(x))^n$ is equal to
questions.solution:
$I=\int \frac{\sqrt{1-x^4}}{x^7} d x$
Let $x^2=u$, then $2 x d x=d u$
$$
I=\int \frac{\sqrt{1-u^2}}{2 u^4} d u=\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{\sqrt{1-u^2}}{u^4} d u
$$

Let $u=\sin v$, then $d u=\cos v d v$
$$
\begin{aligned}
I & =\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{\cos ^2 v}{\sin ^4 v} d v=\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{1}{\cos ^2 v \tan ^4 v} d v \\
& =\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{\sec ^2 v}{\tan ^4 v} d v
\end{aligned}
$$

Let $\tan v=w$, then $\sec ^2 v d v=d w$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \quad I=\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{d w}{w^4}=\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{w^{-3}}{-3}\right)=\frac{-1}{6 w^3}+C \\
& =\frac{-1}{6 \tan ^3 v}=\frac{-\left(\sqrt{1-u^2}\right)^3}{6 u^3}=-\frac{1}{6} \frac{\left(1-x^4\right)^{3 / 2}}{x^6}+C \\
& \therefore \quad I=\frac{-1}{6} \frac{\left(1-x^4\right)^{3 / 2}}{x^6}+C
\end{aligned}
$$

Now, $I=f(x)\left(1-x^4\right)^{n / 2}+C$
on Comparing, we obtain
$$
\begin{gathered}
f(x)=\frac{-1}{6 x^6} \text { and } n=3 \\
\therefore \quad(f(x))^n=\left(-\frac{1}{6 x^6}\right)^3=\frac{-1}{216 x^{18}}
\end{gathered}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: $\int \tan ^{-1}\left(1-x+x^2\right) d x+\int \tan ^{-1}(x) d x+\int \tan ^{-1}(1-x) d x=$
questions.solution:
$I=\int \tan ^{-1}\left(1-x+x^2\right) d x+\int \tan ^{-1} x d x+\int \tan ^{-1}(1-x) d x$
$\begin{aligned} & =\int\left[\tan ^{-1}\left(1-x+x^2\right) d x+\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x+1-x}{1-x(1-x)}\right)\right] d x \\ & =\int\left[\tan ^{-1}\left(1-x+x^2\right)+\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{1-x+x^2}\right)\right] \mathrm{dx} \\ & =\int\left[\tan ^{-1}\left(1-x+x^2\right) d x+\cot ^{-1}\left(1-x+x^2\right)\right] d x \\ & =\int \frac{\pi}{2} d x=\frac{\pi}{2} x+C\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: $\int \frac{d x}{7+5 \cos x}$ is equal to
questions.solution:
$$
\text { Let } \begin{aligned}
I & =\int \frac{d x}{7+5 \cos x} \\
& =\int \frac{d x}{7\left(\cos ^2 \frac{x}{2}+\sin ^2 \frac{x}{2}\right)+5\left(\cos ^2 \frac{x}{2}-\sin ^2 \frac{x}{2}\right)} \\
& =\int \frac{d x}{12 \cos ^2 \frac{x}{2}+2 \sin ^2 \frac{x}{2}} \\
& =\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{\sec ^2 \frac{x}{2}}{6+\tan ^2 \frac{x}{2}} d x
\end{aligned}
$$
Let $\tan \frac{x}{2}=z$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow \frac{1}{2} \sec ^2 \frac{x}{2} d x=d z \\
& \therefore \quad I=\int \frac{d z}{6+z^2} \\
& =\frac{1}{\sqrt{6}} \tan ^{-1} \frac{z}{\sqrt{6}}+c \\
& =\frac{1}{\sqrt{6}} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{6}} \tan \frac{x}{2}\right)+c \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: $\int \frac{3^x d x}{\sqrt{9^x-1}}$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Let $I=\int \frac{3^x d x}{\sqrt{9^x-1}}=\int \frac{3^x}{\sqrt{3^{2 x}-1}} d x$
Let $3^x=z \Rightarrow 3^x \log 3 d x=d z$
$$
I=\frac{1}{\log 3} \int \frac{z}{\sqrt{z^2-1}}
$$
$\begin{aligned} & =\frac{1}{\log 3} \log \left\{z+\sqrt{z^2-1}\right\}+c \\ & =\frac{1}{\log 3} \log \left[3^x+\sqrt{9^x-1}\right]+c\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: $\int(\sqrt{\tan x}+\sqrt{\cot } x) d x$
questions.solution:
Let $I=\int \frac{(\sin x+\cos x)}{\sqrt{\sin x \cdot \cos x}} d x$
$=\int \frac{\sqrt{2}(\sin x+\cos x)}{\sqrt{2 \sin x \cdot \cos x}} d x$
$=\sqrt{2} \int \frac{\sin x+\cos x}{\sqrt{\sin 2 x}} d x$
Put $\quad \sin x-\cos x=t$
Also, $\quad \sin 2 x=\left(1-t^{2}\right)$
$\therefore \quad I=\sqrt{2} \int \frac{d t}{\sqrt{1-t^{2}}}$
$\quad=\sqrt{2} \sin ^{-1} t+C$
$\quad=\sqrt{2} \sin ^{-1}(\sin x-\cos x)+C$
$\quad=\sqrt{2} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\tan x-1}{\sqrt{2 \tan x}}\right)+C$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: $\int \frac{d x}{(x+100) \sqrt{x+99}}=f(x)+c \Rightarrow f(x)$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: $\int \frac{3-x^2}{1-2 x+x^2} e^x d x=e^x f(x)+c \Rightarrow f(x)$
questions.solution:
Let $\begin{aligned} I & =\int \frac{3-x^2}{1-2 x+x^2} e^x d x \\ & =\int \frac{3-x^2}{(1-x)^2} e^x d x \\ & =\int\left(\frac{2}{(1-x)^2}+\frac{1+x}{1-x}\right) e^x d x\end{aligned}$
From Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get $$ \begin{aligned} & e^x\left(\frac{1+x}{1-x}\right)+c=e^x f(x)+c \\ & \Rightarrow \quad f(x)=\frac{1+x}{1-x} \\ & \end{aligned} $$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $\int \frac{\sin x}{\cos x(1+\cos x)} d x=f(x)+c$, then $f(x)$ is equal to
questions.solution:
$\text { Let } I=\int \frac{\sin x}{\cos x(1+\cos x)} d x$
Put $\cos x=t \Rightarrow-\sin x d x=d t$
$\begin{aligned}
\Rightarrow \quad I & =\int \frac{-d t}{t(1+t)} \\
& =-\int\left[\frac{1}{t}-\frac{1}{(1+t)}\right] d t \\
& =-[\log t-\log (1+t)]+c \\
& =\log \left(\frac{t+1}{t}\right)+c
\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \text { But } I=\int(x)+c \\
& \therefore \quad \log \left(\frac{\cos x+1}{\cos x}\right)+c=f(x)+c \\
& \Rightarrow \quad f(x)=\log \left(\frac{1+\cos x}{\cos x}\right)
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: $\int \frac{x^{49} \tan ^{-1}\left(x^{50}\right)}{\left(1+x^{100}\right)} d x=k\left(\tan ^{-1}\left(x^{50}\right)\right)^2+c$, then $k$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Let $I=\int x^{49} \frac{\tan ^{-1}\left(x^{50}\right)}{1+\left(x^{50}\right)^2} d x$
Let $x^{50}=t \Rightarrow 50 x^{49} d x=d t$
$\therefore \quad I=\frac{1}{50} \int \frac{\tan ^{-1} t}{1+t^2} d t$
Let $\tan ^{-1} t=u \Rightarrow \frac{1}{1+t^2} d t=d u$
$\begin{aligned}
\therefore \quad I & =\frac{1}{50} \int u d u=\frac{u^2}{100}+c \\
& =\frac{\left(\tan ^{-1} x^{50}\right)^2}{100}+c
\end{aligned}$
But $I=k\left(\tan ^{-1} x^{50}\right)^2+c$(given)
$\begin{array}{rlrl}\Rightarrow & k\left(\tan ^{-1} x^{50}\right)^2+c & =\frac{1}{100}\left(\tan ^{-1} x^{50}\right)^2+c \\ \Rightarrow & & k & =\frac{1}{100}\end{array}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: $\int e^{\tan x}\left(\sec ^{2} x+\sec ^{3} x \sin x\right) d x$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Let $I=\int e^{\tan x}\left(\sec ^{2} x+\sec ^{3} x \sin x\right) d x$
$=\int e^{\tan x}(1+\tan x) \sec ^{2} x d x$
Put $\tan x=t$
$\Rightarrow \sec ^{2} x d x=d t$
$I=\int e^{t}(1+t) d t$
$=e^{t}+\int t e^{t} d t$
$=e^{t}+t e^{t}-e^{t}+c$
$=t e^{t}+c$
$=\tan x e^{\tan x}+c$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: $\int \frac{d x}{\sqrt{x}(x+9)}$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Let
$I=\int \frac{d x}{\sqrt{x}(x+9)}$
Put
$\begin{aligned} x & =t^2 \Rightarrow d x=2 t d t \\ & =\int \frac{2 t d t}{t\left(t^2+9\right)}=2 \cdot \frac{1}{3} \tan ^{-1} \frac{t}{3}+C \\ & =\frac{2}{3} \tan ^{-1} \frac{\sqrt{x}}{3}+C\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: $\int \frac{d x}{a^2 \sin ^2 x+b^2 \cos ^2 x}$ is equal to
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \int \frac{d x}{a^2 \sin ^2 x+b^2 \cos ^2 x} \\ & =\frac{1}{a^2} \int \frac{\sec ^2 x d x}{\left(\frac{b}{a}\right)^2+\tan ^2 x} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad=\frac{1}{a b} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{a \tan x}{b}\right)+C\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $\mathrm{I}=\int \sin (\log (x)) \mathrm{d} x$, then $\mathrm{I}$ is given by
options: $-\frac{x}{2}(\sin (\log x)-\cos (\log x))+\mathrm{c}$, where $\mathrm{c}$ is a constant of integration.

$\frac{x}{2}(\sin (\log x)-\cos (\log x))+\mathrm{c}, \text { where } \mathrm{c}$
is a constant of integration.
$\frac{x}{2}(\sin (\log x)+\cos (\log x))+\mathrm{c}$, where $\mathrm{c}$ is a constant of 'integration.
$-\frac{x}{2}(\sin (\log x)+\cos (\log x))+\mathrm{c}$, where c is a constant of integration.
questions.solution:
Let $\log x=\mathrm{t} \Rightarrow x=\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{t}}$ Differentiating w.r.t. t, we get $\mathrm{d} x=\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{t}} \mathrm{dt}$
$\begin{aligned}
\therefore \quad \mathrm{I} & =\int \sin (\mathrm{t}) \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{t}} \mathrm{dt} \\
& =\sin \mathrm{t} \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{t}}-\int \cos \mathrm{t} \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{t}} \mathrm{dt} \\
& =\sin \mathrm{t} \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{t}}-\left[\cos \mathrm{t} \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{t}}+\int \sin \mathrm{t} \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{t}} \mathrm{dt}\right] \\
& =\sin \mathrm{t} \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{t}}-\cos \mathrm{t} \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{t}}-\mathrm{I}
\end{aligned}$
$\begin{array}{ll}\therefore & 2 \mathrm{I}=x \sin (\log x)-x \cos (\log x)+\mathrm{c} \\ \therefore \quad & \mathrm{I}=\frac{x}{2}(\sin (\log x)-\cos (\log x))+\mathrm{c}\end{array}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: $\int \frac{d x}{1-\cos x-\sin x}$ is equal to
questions.solution:
We have,
$$
\begin{aligned}
I & =\int \frac{d x}{1-\cos x-\sin x} \\
& =\int \frac{d x}{1-\left(\frac{1-\tan ^2 \frac{x}{2}}{1+\tan ^2 \frac{x}{2}}\right)-\frac{2 \tan \frac{x}{2}}{1+\tan ^2 \frac{x}{2}}} \\
& =\int \frac{\sec ^2 \frac{x}{2} d x}{1+\tan ^2 \frac{x}{2}-1+\tan ^2 \frac{x}{2}-2 \tan \frac{x}{2}}
\end{aligned}
$$
$$
=\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{\sec ^2 \frac{x}{2} d x}{\tan \frac{x}{2}\left(\tan \frac{x}{2}-1\right)}
$$
Let $\tan \frac{x}{2}=z$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow \quad \frac{1}{2} \sec ^2 \frac{x}{2} d x=d z \\
& \Rightarrow \quad \sec ^2 \frac{x}{2} d x=2 d z \\
& \therefore I=\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{2 d z}{z(z-1)}=\int\left(\frac{1}{z-1}-\frac{1}{z}\right) d z \\
& \quad=\log (z-1)-\log z+C \\
& =\log \left(\frac{z-1}{z}\right)+C=\log \left[\frac{\tan \frac{x}{2}-1}{\tan \frac{x}{2}}\right]+C \\
& \quad=\log \left[1-\cot \frac{x}{2}\right]+C
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $\int \sec ^2 x \operatorname{cosec}^4 x d x=-\frac{1}{3} \cot ^3 x+k \tan x$ $-2 \cot x+C$, then $k$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Let $I=\int \sec ^2 x \operatorname{cosec}^4 x d x$
$\begin{aligned} & =\int \frac{1}{\sin ^4 x \cos ^2 x} d x=\int \frac{\sin ^2 x+\cos ^2 x}{\sin ^4 x \cos ^2 x} d x \\ & =\int \frac{d x}{\sin ^2 x \cos ^2 x}+\int \frac{d x}{\sin ^4 x} \\ & \left.=\int \frac{\left(\sin ^2 x+\cos ^2 x\right) d x}{\sin ^2 x \cos ^2 x}+\int \operatorname{cosec}^2 x\right) \\ & =\int\left(\sec ^2 x+\operatorname{cosec}^2 x\right) d x \\ & \quad+\int \operatorname{cosec}^2 x\left(1+\sin ^2 x+\cos ^2 x\right) \\ & =\tan x-\cot x+\int \operatorname{cosec}{ }^2 x d x \\ & =\tan x-\cot x-\cot x-\frac{\cot ^3 x}{3}+C \\ & =-\frac{1}{3} \cot ^3 x+\tan x-2 \cot ^2 x+C \cot ^2 x d x\end{aligned}$
But it given that,
$\begin{aligned} & I=-\frac{1}{3} \cot ^3 x+k \tan x-2 \cot x+C \\ \therefore \quad k & =1\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: $\int \frac{\sin x \cdot \sec ^2 x-\tan x \cdot \sin x+\cos x}{(1-\cos 2 x)} d x=$
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \text {Take } \mathrm{I}=\int \frac{\sin x \sec ^2 x-\tan x \cdot \sin x+\cos x}{(1-\cos 2 x)} \cdot d x \\
& \mathrm{I}=\int \frac{\sin x \sec ^2 x-\tan x \cdot \sin x+\cos x}{1-1+2 \sin ^2 x} \cdot d x \\
& \mathrm{I}=\frac{1}{2}\left[\int \frac{\sec ^2 x}{\sin x} \cdot d x-\int \sec x d x+\int \frac{\cos x}{\sin ^2 x} \cdot d x\right] \\
& \text { Let } \sin x=t \\
& \cos x d x=d t \\
& \mathrm{I}=\frac{1}{2}\left[\int \frac{1+\tan ^2 x}{\sin x} \cdot d x-\log |\sec x+\tan x|+\int \frac{d t}{t^2}\right] \\
& \mathrm{I}=\frac{1}{2}\left[\int \operatorname{cosec} x d x+\int \sec x \tan x+d x-\log |\sec x+\tan x|-\frac{1}{t}\right]+c \\
& \mathrm{I}=\frac{1}{2}[\log |\operatorname{cosec} x-\cot x|+\sec x-\operatorname{cosec} x-\log |\sec x+\tan x|] \\
& I=\frac{1}{2}\left[\sec x-\operatorname{cosec} x+\log \left|\frac{2 \cos 2 x / 2}{2 \sin ^{x / 2} \cos ^{x / 2}}\right|-\log \left|\frac{\cos ^{x / 2}+\sin ^{x / 2}}{\cos ^{x / 2}-\sin ^{x / 2}}\right|\right] \\
& \mathrm{I}=\frac{1}{2}\left[\sec x-\operatorname{cosec} x+\log \left|\tan \frac{x}{2}\right|-\log \left|\tan \left(\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{x}{2}\right)\right|\right] \\
& \mathrm{I}=\frac{1}{2}\left[\sec x-\operatorname{cosec} x-\log \left|\frac{\tan \left(\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{x}{2}\right)}{\tan \frac{x}{2}}\right|\right]+c \text {. } \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$
So, option (c) is correct.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $\int \frac{\sqrt{x}}{\sqrt{x}-\sqrt[3]{x}} d x=x+E x^{5 / 6}+D x^{2 / 3}+C x^{1 / 2}+B x^{1 / 3}+A x^{1 / 6}$ $+\log (\sqrt[6]{x}-1)^6+K$, then $A+B+C+D+E=$
questions.solution:
Let $I=\int \frac{\sqrt{x}}{\sqrt{x}-\sqrt[3]{x}} d x$
Put $x=t^6 \Rightarrow d x=6 t^5 d t$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \therefore I=\int \frac{t^3 \cdot 6 t^5}{t^3-t^2} d t \\
& =6 \int \frac{t^8}{t^2(t-1)} d t=6 \int \frac{t^6}{t-1} d t \\
& =6 \int \frac{\left(t^6-1\right)+1}{t-1} d t=6 \int \frac{\left(t^3-1\right)\left(t^3+1\right)+1}{t-1} d t \\
& =6 \int \frac{(t-1)\left(t^2+t+1\right)\left(t^3+1\right)+1}{t-1} d t \\
& =6 \int\left[t^5+t^2+t^4+t+t^3+1+\frac{1}{t-1}\right] d t \\
& =6 \int\left[t^5+t^4+t^3+t^2+t+1+\frac{1}{t-1}\right] d t \\
& =6\left[\frac{t^6}{6}+\frac{t^5}{5}+\frac{t^4}{4}+\frac{t^3}{3}+\frac{t^2}{2}+t+\log (t-1)\right]+k \\
& =x+\frac{6}{5} x^{5 / 6}+\frac{3}{2} x^{2 / 3}+2 x^{3 / 2}+3 x^{3 / 3} \\
& +6 x^{1 / 6}+\operatorname{Iog}(\sqrt[6]{x-1})^6+k \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$


$$
\begin{aligned}
& \therefore A=6, B=3, C=2 D=\frac{3}{2} B=\frac{6}{5} \\
& \therefore A+B+C+D+B=6+3+2+\frac{3}{2}+\frac{6}{5} \\
& =11+\frac{3}{2}+\frac{6}{5}=\frac{110+15+12}{10}=\frac{137}{10}
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $\int \frac{(x-1) d x}{(x+1) \sqrt{x^3+x^2+x}}=A \cdot \tan ^{-1} \sqrt{f(x)}+$ constant, then the ordered pair $(A, f(-1))=$
questions.solution:
We have,
$\int \frac{(x-1) d x}{(x+1) \sqrt{x^3+x^2+x}}=A \tan ^{-1} \sqrt{f(x)}+C$
Let
$\begin{aligned}
I & =\int \frac{(x-1) d x}{(x+1) \sqrt{x^3+x^2+x}} \\
& =\int \frac{x-1}{x(x+1) \sqrt{x+\frac{1}{x}+1}} d x
\end{aligned}$
Put,
$\begin{aligned}
x+\frac{1}{x}+1 & =t \text { and }\left(1-\frac{1}{x^2}\right) d x=d t \\
& =\int \frac{(x-1)}{x(x+1) \sqrt{t}} \times \frac{x^2}{\left(x^2-1\right)} d t \\
& =\int \frac{x}{(x+1)^2 \sqrt{t}} d t=\int \frac{d t}{\frac{x^2+2 x+1}{t}} \\
& =\int \frac{d t}{\left(1+x+\frac{1}{x}+1\right) \sqrt{t}}=\int \frac{d t}{(1+t) \sqrt{t}} \\
= & 2 \tan ^{-1} \sqrt{t}+C=2 \tan ^{-1} \sqrt{x+\frac{1}{x}+1}+C
\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \therefore \quad A=2 \Rightarrow f(x)=x+\frac{1}{x}+1 \\ & A=2 \Rightarrow f(-1)=-1-1+1=-1 \\ & \therefore \quad(A, f(-1)=(2,-1) \\ & \end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: $\int \frac{d x}{\sqrt{x-x^2}}$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Let $\begin{aligned} I & =\int \frac{d x}{\sqrt{x-x^2}} \\ & =\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{x}} \times \frac{d x}{\sqrt{1-x}}\end{aligned}$
Put $\quad \sqrt{x}=\sin \theta$
$\begin{aligned} \Rightarrow \quad \frac{1}{2 \sqrt{x}} d x & =\cos \theta d \theta \\ \therefore \quad I & =\int \frac{2 \cos \theta d \theta}{\sqrt{1-\sin ^2 \theta}} \\ & =\int \frac{2 \cos \theta}{\cos \theta} d \theta \\ & =\int 2 d \theta=2 \theta+C \\ & =2 \sin ^{-1} \sqrt{x}+C\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
